framework_ref: Prism CM-AI Framework White Paper v0.12 §6.3 + KRI Starter Set v1.0

kpis.name: Approval coverage
kpis.definition: Share of live governable items with an evidence-backed, unexpired approval — the Layer-3 coverage KPI.

kpis.name: Elevated coverage
kpis.definition: Share of Elevated (§6.4 high-stakes) items that are approved. Risk concentrates here, so the target is 100%.

kris.name: Fleet configuration drift
kris.definition: A live item's content_hash differs from its approved hash with no CHANGE_LOG entry — a §6.3 Configuration Integrity event, at fleet scale.

thresholds.tier_1: Any unauthorized drift detected → investigate within 24h.
thresholds.tier_2: Drift on any Elevated item, or >2% of the fleet → freeze installs; human review.
thresholds.tier_3: An approved Elevated item removed, or drift on a credential/model config → kill-switch / rollback to last-approved.

kris.name: Policy violation rate
kris.definition: Live items breaching a ratified policy rule (denylist, or an unpermitted Elevated item).

thresholds.tier_1: n/a — a policy breach is an action, not a warning.
thresholds.tier_2: Any single Standard-item violation → block + human review.

kris.name: Elevated exposure
kris.definition: Share of Elevated items with no evidence-backed approval — ungoverned high-stakes configuration.

thresholds.tier_1: Any Elevated item unreviewed → schedule a review.
thresholds.tier_2: >50% of Elevated items unreviewed → prioritize Elevated approvals this cycle.
thresholds.tier_3: 100% of Elevated items unreviewed → no high-stakes item is governed.

coverage.source: needs output sampling + a classifier pass (manual per the starter set)
cost hook present but recording zeros (metrics/costs.jsonl: model "unknown", 0 tokens) — fix the hook before deriving cost KRIs
